blocks.body: Passive with infinitives and gerunds are complex constructions used in formal and academic styles. In this topic, you will learn the forms to be done, being done, having been done and the most frequent patterns of their use.

{ "title": "9. Short summary", "items": [ "to be + V3 (passive infinitive): The report needs to be finished.", "to have been + V3 (perfect passive infinitive): He seems to have been promoted.", "being + V3 (passive gerund): She hates being interrupted.", "having been + V3 (perfect passive gerund): Having been warned...", "Perfect forms indicate a previous action.", "Use being + V3 after prepositions and verbs with -ing.", "These forms are typical of academic and formal style." ] }
